rankings.title: Follow the Money or Follow the Crime? Police Resource Reallocation After Civil Asset Forfeiture Reform
conditions: restricting the core analysis to clean pre-NIBRS years
demonstrating stable reporting
pre-trends
making substitution toward serious-crime enforcement/clearances central rather than relying only on drug-arrest effects

rankings.title: Can Shorter Workweeks Save Fertility? South Korea's 52-Hour Reform and the Marriage-Birth Response
conditions: centering the paper on wave 1
establishing a strong hours first stage
treating births as secondary unless better individual-level or administrative data can be obtained

rankings.title: Does Legal Sports Betting Cannibalize State Lottery Revenue? Staggered Legalization and Fiscal Substitution
conditions: obtaining higher-frequency or product-level lottery data
exploiting online rollout more sharply
linking the revenue effects directly to education-funding consequences
